titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert A. Johnson, Royal Mint to Edward Carter Preston, 20 January 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert A. Johnson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
730 PRE 4/7/1 ROYAL MINT, LONDON, E.1. 20th January, 1923. Dear Carter Preston,
As Engraver of His Majesty's Seals, I have been
instructed to submit designs for a Great Seal for the
new Government of Northern Ireland, and the Committee
appointed by H.M. the King to advise me on such subjects
has suggested that it would be well, in the first place,
to invite the ideas of a select number of artists
interested in this kind of work. The general treatment recommended is; for
the Seal itself, H.M. King Georve V. on horseback, with
His Royal Style and Titles in Latin on the margin; and,
for the counterseal, a strictly heraldic treatment, i.e.
the Royal Arms with an escutcheon over them showing
the Cross of Ulster. The Seal should be circular and
5 in diameter, and the treatment not to be over
elaborate as we think has been the case with the more
recent of the Great Seals of England. I should be glad to lear from you whether
you would be prepared to submit sketches; or, if preferred
Carter Preston Esq. models
models for a Seal and Counterseal on these lines. I should
add that the Committee thought that it would be fair to
offer a fee of then guineas to artists who agreed to submit
such a designs. Believe me, Yours sincerely, Robert A Johnson
